codeSystems.name: IdentifierUse

codeSystems.description: Identifies the purpose for this identifier, if known .

codeSystems.name: LinkType
codeSystems.title: LinkType

codeSystems.name: ObservationStatus
codeSystems.title: ObservationStatus

codeSystems.name: LOINC
codeSystems.title: Logical Observation Identifiers, Names and Codes (LOINC)
codeSystems.description: LOINC provides a set of universal names and ID codes for identifying laboratory and clinical test results.1,2 LOINC facilitates the exchange and pooling of results, such as blood hemoglobin, serum potassium, or vital signs, for clinical care, outcomes management, and research. LOINC's universal identifiers (names and codes) can be used in the context of order and observation exchanges between information systems that use syntax standards such as HL73, CEN TC251, ISO TC215, ASTM4, and DICOM. Specifically, the identifier can be used as the coded value for an observation in any other standard that uses the observation/observation value paradigm, whether messages, documents, application programming interface (API), etc. For example, LOINC codes are used widely in the OBX segment Observation Identifier field (OBX-3) of an ORU HL7 (HL7 version 2.x or ASTM 1238-9410) message that may be sent between a Clinical Laboratory Information Management Systems (LIMS) and Electronic Health Record Systems (EHR).5, 6 In this way, LOINC codes provide universal identifiers that allow the exchange of clinical data between heterogeneous computing environments.

codeSystems.name: SNOMED_CT
codeSystems.title: SNOMED CT (all versions)
codeSystems.description: SNOMED CT is the most comprehensive and precise clinical health terminology product in the world, owned and distributed around the world by The International Health Terminology Standards Development Organisation (IHTSDO).
codeSystems.http://hl7.org/fhir/R4/valueset-referenc: Observation Reference Range Applies To Codes

codeSystems.name: AdmitSource
codeSystems.title: Admit source
codeSystems.description: This value set defines a set of codes that can be used to indicate from where the patient came in.

codeSystems.name: DataAbsentReason
codeSystems.title: DataAbsentReason
codeSystems.description: Used to specify why the normally expected content of the data element is missing.

codeSystems.name: DiagnosisRole
codeSystems.title: Diagnosis Role
codeSystems.description: This value set defines a set of codes that can be used to express the role of a diagnosis on the Encounter or EpisodeOfCare record.

codeSystems.name: Diet
codeSystems.title: Diet
codeSystems.description: This value set defines a set of codes that can be used to indicate dietary preferences or restrictions a patient may have.

codeSystems.name: DischargeDisposition
codeSystems.title: Discharge disposition
codeSystems.description: This value set defines a set of codes that can be used to where the patient left the hospital.

codeSystems.name: SpecialArrangements
codeSystems.title: Special arrangements
codeSystems.description: This value set defines a set of codes that can be used to indicate the kinds of special arrangements in place for a patients visit.

codeSystems.name: EncounterType
codeSystems.title: Encounter type
codeSystems.description: This example value set defines a set of codes that can be used to indicate the type of encounter: a specific code indicating type of service provided.

codeSystems.name: EpisodeOfCareType
codeSystems.title: Episode of care type
codeSystems.description: This example value set defines a set of codes that can be used to express the usage type of an EpisodeOfCare record.

codeSystems.name: LocationType
codeSystems.title: Location type
codeSystems.description: This example value set defines a set of codes that can be used to indicate the physical form of the Location.This includes several 'non physical' codes which are still considered a location.

codeSystems.name: ObservationCategoryCodes
codeSystems.title: Observation Category Codes
codeSystems.description: Observation Category codes.

codeSystems.name: ParticipantType
codeSystems.title: Participant type
codeSystems.description: This value set defines a set of codes that can be used to indicate how an individual participates in an encounter.

codeSystems.name: ObservationReferenceRangeMeaningCodes
codeSystems.title: Observation Reference Range Meaning Codes
codeSystems.description: This value set defines a set of codes that can be used to indicate the meaning/use of a reference range for a particular target population.

codeSystems.name: ServiceType
codeSystems.title: Service type
codeSystems.description: This value set defines an example set of codes of service-types.

codeSystems.name: ReAdmissionIndicator
codeSystems.title: re-admissionIndicator
codeSystems.description: Code system of concepts which are used to specify that a patient is being re admitted to a healthcare facilityin from which they were discharged, and indicates the circumstances around such re-admission.  Used in HL7 Version 2.x messagine in the PV1 segment.

codeSystems.name: ContactRole2
codeSystems.title: contactRole2
codeSystems.description: Code system of concepts which specify a relationship role that the next of kin/associated parties plays with regard to the patient. Also used in referrals, for example, it may be necessary to identify the contact representative at the clinic that sent a referral. Used in HL7 Version 2 messaging in the NK1 and CTD segments after 2.5, when it replace 2.16.840.1.113883.18.57.

codeSystems.name: IdentifierType
codeSystems.title: identifierType
codeSystems.description: HL7-defined code system of concepts specifying type of identifier. Used in HL7 Version 2.x messaging data types CX, PLN, PPN, XCN and XON.

codeSystems.name: ActCode
codeSystems.title: ActCode
codeSystems.description:
A code specifying the particular kind of Act that the Act-instance represents within its class.

*Constraints:* The kind of Act (e.g. physical examination, serum potassium, inpatient encounter, charge financial transaction, etc.) is specified with a code from one of several, typically external, coding systems. The coding system will depend on the class of Act, such as LOINC for observations, etc.

Conceptually, the Act.code must be a specialization of the Act.classCode. This is why the structure of ActClass domain should be reflected in the superstructure of the ActCode domain and then individual codes or externally referenced vocabularies subordinated under these domains that reflect the ActClass structure.

Act.classCode and Act.code are not modifiers of each other but the Act.code concept should really imply the Act.classCode concept. For a negative example, it is not appropriate to use an Act.code "potassium" together with and Act.classCode for "laboratory observation" to somehow mean "potassium laboratory observation" and then use the same Act.code for "potassium" together with Act.classCode for "medication" to mean "substitution of potassium". This mutually modifying use of Act.code and Act.classCode is not permitted.

codeSystems.name: ActPriority
codeSystems.title: ActPriority
codeSystems.description: A set of codes (e.g., for routine, emergency), specifying the urgency under which the Act happened, can happen, is happening, is intended to happen, or is requested/demanded to happen.

codeSystems.name: EncounterSpecialCourtesy
codeSystems.title: EncounterSpecialCourtesy
codeSystems.description: **** MISSING DESCRIPTION ****

codeSystems.title: MaritalStatus
codeSystems.description:

codeSystems.name: NullFlavor
codeSystems.title: NullFlavor
codeSystems.description: A collection of codes specifying why a valid value is not present.

codeSystems.name: ObservationInterpretation
codeSystems.title: ObservationInterpretation
codeSystems.description: One or more codes providing a rough qualitative interpretation of the observation, such as "normal" / "abnormal", "low" / "high", "better" / "worse", "resistant" / "susceptible", "expected" / "not expected". The value set is intended to be for ANY use where coded representation of an interpretation is needed.

codeSystems.name: ParticipationType
codeSystems.title: ParticipationType
codeSystems.description: A code specifying the meaning and purpose of every Participation instance. Each of its values implies specific constraints on the Roles undertaking the participation.

codeSystems.name: CRAddressCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Address
codeSystems.description: Address codes containing placeholder values that must be customised prior to implementation to reflect the administrative geography of the country or region. The recommended approach uses text-type data elements combined with dependent dropdown lists, aligning with the CanReg5 convention where the address variable is two characters in length encoding a combination of two administrative levels.

codeSystems.name: CRBasisDiagnosisCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Basis diagnosis
codeSystems.description: Basis of diagnosis codes aligned with the CanReg5 basis dictionary. Codes 0-4 represent non-microscopically confirmed diagnoses (death certificate only, clinical, clinical investigation/ultrasound, surgery/autopsy, laboratory test). Codes 5-8 represent microscopically confirmed diagnoses (cytology, histology of metastasis, histology of primary, autopsy with histology). Code 9 represents unknown basis. Used in the Basis data quality check.

codeSystems.name: CRBehaviourCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Behaviour
codeSystems.description: Tumour behaviour codes aligned with the CanReg5 ICD-O-3.2 behaviour dictionary. Used in the Tumor stage to classify the biological behaviour of the neoplasm. These codes are referenced in data quality checks including the Topography-Behaviour and Sex-Morphology checks. Codes must not be modified as they are mapped directly to CanReg5 standards.

codeSystems.name: CRGradeCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Grade
codeSystems.description: Tumour grade and differentiation codes aligned with the CanReg5 ICD-O-3.2 grade dictionary. Codes 1-4 represent histological grade (well differentiated to undifferentiated/anaplastic). Codes 5-8 are reserved for lymphomas and leukaemias (T-cell, B-cell, Null cell, NK cell). Code 9 represents unknown/not stated. Grade is mandatory only when behaviour is malignant (3). These codes are used in the Grade data quality check.

codeSystems.name: CRMorphology4CS
codeSystems.title: CR - Morphology4
codeSystems.description: ICD-O-3.2 morphology codes (first four digits) mapped from the CanReg5 morphology dictionary (morphology4.tsv). These codes represent the histological type of the neoplasm. Since the field is configured as a closed option set, validity of morphology values is guaranteed by the data entry interface. Codes must not be modified as they are mapped directly to CanReg5 standards and used in multiple data quality checks.

codeSystems.name: CRMorphologyFamilyCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Morphology Family
codeSystems.description: Morphological family grouping codes derived from the CanReg5 MorphFam.txt lookup file. Each morphology code is assigned to a morphological family, which is used in the Sex-Morphology check (to identify rare sex-morphology combinations) and the Topography-Morphology check (to evaluate morphology-topography combinations against MUST and MUST-NOT lookup tables). Code 99 (N/A) indicates the family is accepted with any topography.

codeSystems.name: CRMorphologyGroupCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Morphology Group
codeSystems.description: Morphology group codes used in the Multiple Primary Tester data quality check. Groups are derived from morphology codes using the mapping logic defined in the CanReg5 DefaultMultiplePrimaryTester class. Groups 8-14 represent haematopoietic/lymphoid tissue subtypes (systemic groups). Group 0 indicates an invalid morphology, and group 17 indicates an unspecified morphology.

codeSystems.name: CRMorphologyResultCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Morphology result
codeSystems.description: Intermediate morphology evaluation result codes used in the Multiple Primary Tester check. When the morphology comparison between two tumours is inconclusive (undecided), the check proceeds to topography evaluation. 'Invalid' indicates one of the morphology groups is invalid (0). 'Go to topography' indicates the morphology evaluation was undecided and topography comparison is needed.

codeSystems.name: CRMultiplePrimaryTestCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Multiple primary test
codeSystems.description: Result codes for the Multiple Primary Tester data quality check. This check determines whether two or more tumours registered for the same patient represent duplicate registrations of the same tumour or distinct multiple primary tumours, based on the CanReg5 DefaultMultiplePrimaryTester logic.
codeSystems.ValueSet-CRMultiplePrimaryTestVS.html: CR - Multiple primary test value set
codeSystems.ValueSet-TumorQuestionnaire_CRMultiplePr: CR - Multiple primary test value set

codeSystems.name: CRPlaceholderSourceTypeCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Placeholder: Source type
codeSystems.description: Placeholder source type codes for the Source stage. These values must be customised prior to implementation to reflect the source types relevant to the local registry context (e.g. hospital records, pathology laboratory reports, death certificates).
codeSystems.ValueSet-CRPlaceholderSourceTypeVS.html: CR - Placeholder: Source type value set
codeSystems.ValueSet-SourceQuestionnaire_CRPlacehold: CR - Placeholder: Source type value set

codeSystems.name: CRSexCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Sex
codeSystems.description: Sex codes aligned with the CanReg5 sex dictionary, using numeric codes. These codes are referenced in multiple data quality checks throughout the program, including the Sex-Topography and Sex-Morphology checks. Any modification to these codes or substitution with a differently coded option set would break the logic of those checks.

codeSystems.name: CRSiteCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Site
codeSystems.description: Anatomical site codes used to filter topography values in a dependent dropdown list. When a site is selected, only the topography codes valid for the chosen site are presented for selection, simplifying data entry for registry staff.

codeSystems.name: CRTopographyCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Topography
codeSystems.description: ICD-O-3 topography codes mapped from the CanReg5 topography dictionary (topography.tsv). These codes represent the anatomical site of the tumour origin. The topography field is implemented as a dependent dropdown list filtered by the selected Site. Codes must not be modified as they are mapped directly to CanReg5 standards and used in multiple data quality checks.

codeSystems.name: CRTopographyGroupCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Topography Group
codeSystems.description: Topography group codes used in the Multiple Primary Tester data quality check. Groups are derived from topography codes using the mapping logic defined in the CanReg5 DefaultMultiplePrimaryTester class. Group 80 indicates an unspecified topography. These groups determine whether tumours at different sites should be considered duplicate or multiple primaries.

codeSystems.name: CRTopographyMorphologyKeyCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Topography Morphology key
codeSystems.description: Keys used in the Topography-Morphology data quality check to determine how each morphological family relates to topography. NA (*): the family is accepted with any topography. Plus (+): the family has a restricted set of expected topography sites checked against the MUST list. Minus (-): the family has a restricted set of prohibited topography sites checked against the MUST-NOT list.
codeSystems.ValueSet-CRTopographyMorphologyKeyVS.htm: CR - Topography Morphology key value set
codeSystems.ValueSet-TumorQuestionnaire_CRTopography: CR - Topography Morphology key value set

codeSystems.name: CRTumorNumberCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Tumor number
codeSystems.description: Tumor number codes used as a unique identifier for each tumor within the registry. Combined with the Patient ID, the Tumor Number constitutes the Tumor ID (the composite identifier that uniquely identifies a tumor record). A validation mechanism prevents the same tumor number from being assigned to more than one tumor belonging to the same patient.

codeSystems.name: CRVitalStatusCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Vital status
codeSystems.description: Vital status codes used in the Follow-up stage to record the current status of the registered patient. When the status is 'Died', the date of death must also be recorded.

codeSystems.name: CREncounterTypeCS
codeSystems.title: Cancer Registry Encounter Types
codeSystems.description: Encounter types representing the three repeatable program stages of the DHIS2 Cancer Registry tracker program.
codeSystems.ValueSet-cr-encounter-type-vs.html: Cancer Registry Encounter Types Value Set

codeSystems.name: CRObservationCodesCS
codeSystems.title: Cancer Registry Observation Codes
codeSystems.description: Code system defining observation type codes for cancer registry observations. Used where no standard LOINC code exists for cancer-registry-specific concepts.
codeSystems.ValueSet-cr-observation-codes-vs.html: Cancer Registry Observation Codes Value Set

codeSystems.name: Ietf3066
codeSystems.title: Tags for the Identification of Languages
codeSystems.description: Older value from OID registry.  Superceded; see recommendations in BCP-47.
